metadata.source_ar: البنك المركزي السعودي (ساما) + صندوق التنمية الصناعية السعودي
metadata.notes: SAMA policy rates, SAIBOR benchmarks, SIDF financing programs, and Monsha'at SME programs as of Q1 2025. Rates subject to SAMA monetary policy decisions.

saibor_rates.notes: Saudi Arabian Interbank Offered Rate (SAIBOR). SAR is pegged to USD at 3.75, so SAIBOR closely tracks US Fed Funds Rate.

sama_policy_rate.notes: SAMA held rates steady in 2024 following the US Fed pause. Rate cuts expected as global inflation normalizes.

bank_lending_rates.notes: Average rates across Saudi commercial banks for standard products

requirements: Operating for minimum 2 years or new industrial project
sidf_programs.description_en: Concessional financing for Saudi SMEs in industrial and productive sectors to support capacity expansion and modernization

requirements: Saudi industrial company with valid industrial license
Project must be in Saudi Arabia
Audited financial statements (3 years for existing companies)
sidf_programs.description_en: Long-term concessional financing for large-scale industrial projects contributing to Vision 2030 industrial diversification goals

requirements: SME with annual revenue below SAR 200 million
Not classified as red in Nitaqat
sidf_programs.description_en: Government-backed loan guarantee program reducing bank risk exposure to enable SME access to commercial bank financing at market rates. Kafalah guarantees up to 80% of loan principal.

requirements: Training completion at approved SIDF center
sidf_programs.description_en: Interest-free micro financing for very small Saudi-owned enterprises and artisans to formalize and scale their businesses

requirements: Must have existing SIDF loan in good standing
sidf_programs.description_en: Supplementary financing for existing SIDF clients seeking to expand capacity or modernize production facilities

monshaat_programs.description_en: Interest-free direct loans from Monsha'at for early-stage Saudi SMEs and startups to fund initial operations and growth

requirements: Saudi-founded startup less than 3 years old
monshaat_programs.description_en: Equity-free accelerator grant program providing mentorship, workspace, and capital to high-potential Saudi startups

requirements: Valid commercial registration or intent to register
monshaat_programs.description_en: Dedicated support program for Saudi female entrepreneurs including interest-free financing, mentoring, and market access support
Microfinance guarantee scheme enabling individual Saudi entrepreneurs to access small loans from licensed microfinance institutions

tax_rates.notes: Saudi tax and zakat rates as of 2025. Saudi nationals and GCC nationals pay Zakat; foreign investors pay Corporate Income Tax.

zakat_percent.applicable_to: Saudi and GCC nationals; Saudi-owned legal entities

corporate_income_tax_foreign.applicable_to: Foreign-owned companies or foreign shareholder portion of mixed ownership
corporate_income_tax_foreign.notes: Saudi or GCC national shareholders pay Zakat at 2.5%; non-GCC foreign shareholders pay CIT at 20% on their proportionate share of income

real_estate_transaction_tax.applicable_to: Sale or transfer of real estate

special_economic_zones.description_en: Saudi Arabia's first industrial SEZ with zero tax, zero VAT, and zero customs for manufacturers

special_economic_zones.name_ar: المنطقة الاقتصادية الخاصة في الملك عبدالله
special_economic_zones.description_en: Integrated city with industrial valley, port, and residential zones offering competitive incentives for investors

special_economic_zones.name_ar: المنطقة الاقتصادية الخاصة في رأس الخير
special_economic_zones.name_en: Ras Al Khair Special Economic Zone
special_economic_zones.description_en: Industrial SEZ focused on mining and metals downstream processing adjacent to major mineral extraction facilities
Flagship giga-project SEZ with separate regulatory framework, targeting advanced technology and sustainable industries
